title: Fukuro toji — Japanese pouch binding
materials: text paper 70-90 gsm, cut to page size (each sheet is folded in half, folded edge out)
sources: Japanese bookbinding tradition documented in Kojiro Ikegami, Japanese Bookbinding (1986 English translation, Weatherhill); this text is still in copyright. Draw on structural description only. Cross-reference yotsume (four-hole stab) stitch as a related PD-canonical structure.
notes: Fukuro toji (pouch binding): each page is a single sheet of paper, folded in half with the fold at the foreedge, sewn through the open edge. The folded edge creates a hidden pocket — traditionally used in Japan to hide the reverse (often blank or printed with unfinished drafts). Covers: sheet preparation (fold and alignment), cover attachment, the four-hole stab sewing pattern used for this structure, trimming the open edge.
